entries.text: First seeds planted. EDEN online. The dome smells like wet soil and possibility.
Spc. Reyes stays late to check the germination trays. Nothing yet, but she can feel it.
First sprouts: spinach pushing through. Tiny green arcs in the growing medium.
Tomato seedlings emerge. Reyes photographs each one and names Tray A 'Los Primeros.'
Potato shoots breaking surface. Soybean cotyledons unfurling. The dome is waking up.
All zones showing green. Basil scenting the dome. Cmdr. Chen says it smells like her grandmother's kitchen.
Spinach rosettes forming. The first real leaves. Dr. Okonkwo measures them daily for the science log.
Tomato plants staked and climbing. Potato canopy thick and green. The dome looks like a garden now.
First tomato flowers open. Spc. Reyes hand-pollinates with a small brush. 'Come on,' she whispers.
Wheat tillers spreading. Soybean nodules visible on roots — nitrogen fixation confirmed. EDEN learning.
FIRST HARVEST. Cmdr. Chen eats fresh spinach for the first time on Mars. She closes her eyes and smiles.
Flight rule #1 written: 'Always stagger plantings. Never bet the harvest on one batch.'
Spinach Batch B harvested. The system works — continuous fresh greens confirmed.
First tiny green tomatoes visible. Reyes says they look like emeralds.
First tomato harvest: 60 kg. Spc. Reyes bites into one and says it tastes like home. The crew cheers.
Stagger tomato harvest: another 60 kg. Dr. Okonkwo makes tomato soup. Best meal since Earth.
First potato harvest: 30 kg. Eng. Volkov roasts them with basil. 'Like my mother's,' he says quietly.
First protein harvest: soybean and lentil. All four zones now producing. EDEN is fully operational.
100 sols of EDEN. 18 flight rules learned. The greenhouse has become the heart of the habitat.
First wheat harvest. Cmdr. Chen grinds flour and makes flatbread. Fresh bread on Mars. 23 flight rules.
Third spinach and second tomato cycles harvested same week. The dome is abundant.
Dr. Okonkwo's health report: crew vitamin C levels optimal, iron levels stable. The greenhouse is working.
Second potato harvest. Volkov experiments with potato soup. The crew votes it 'best recipe so far.'
Second protein cycle complete. Lentil soup night. Okonkwo is happy. 35 flight rules.
Dust storm approaching. Light levels dropping. EDEN compensates with LED boost. All systems nominal.
Storm peak. External dust coating the dome. Inside, the plants don't know. EDEN keeps growing.
Storm passes. External solar panels cleaned by Volkov during EVA. EDEN never missed a beat.
CME WARNING. Coronal mass ejection incoming. 22 minutes to impact. EDEN enters triage mode.
Spc. Reyes runs to the dome. Deploys radiation shields over the vitamin zone. Hands shaking but steady.
CME IMPACT. Radiation spike. Sensors show elevated levels in protein zone. Vitamin zone shielded successfully.
Assessment begins. Soybean Batch A showing stress signs. Lentil Batch A wilting. Tomatoes protected.
EDEN triage decision: sacrifice stressed soybean batch, protect remaining crops, activate reserve microgreens.
Microgreens from reserve zone harvested to cover nutrition gap. 10m2 reserve proves its worth.
Recovery plantings in protein zone. New soybean seeds. Reyes tends them like newborns.
New growth in damaged zone. Recovery ahead of schedule. 47 flight rules now. EDEN adapts.
Eng. Volkov repairs a micro-crack in irrigation Line 3. Caught by EDEN sensors before any crop damage.
Third protein harvest. Yields recovering. The crew exhales.
Tomato Cycle 4 and Spinach Cycle 6 harvested. Over 800 kg total harvested. 58 flight rules.
Sol 300. Two-thirds of the mission complete. The greenhouse has become family. Chen writes home about it.
Potato Cycle 4. Volkov says the potatoes get better every cycle. He's probably right — EDEN is optimizing.
Final planting cycles begin. Every seed counts now. 72 flight rules guiding every decision.
Wheat Cycle 3 harvested. Last bread flour of the mission. Chen makes it count.
Final soybean and lentil harvest. 80 flight rules. Protein zone goes quiet after 376 sols of service.
Spinach Cycle 8. Still producing. The simplest crop, the most reliable. 85 flight rules.
Sol 400. Final stretch. EDEN has produced over 1,100 kg of food. Crew health excellent.
Final potato harvest. Volkov saves one tuber — 'for the journey home.' 92 flight rules.
Last potato stagger harvest. Carb zone completes its mission. Thank you, potatoes.
Crew begins greenhouse decommission prep. Reyes walks the rows one last time, touching every plant.
Final harvests: Spinach and Tomato. Fresh food until nearly the last day. 1,184.5 kg total.
EDEN powers down growing systems. Reyes saves seeds from the best tomato plant. 'For next time,' she says.
Mission complete. 4 humans fed. 312 flight rules learned. EDEN remembers.
